lede: A look at June 19 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Lou Gehrig and Millie Gibson, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Death of Regan Russell
events.desc: Animal rights advocate Regan Russell is run over and killed by a transport truck outside of a pig slaughterhouse in Burlington, Ontario.

events.title: Shishou incident
events.desc: Mass riots involving over 10,000 people and 10,000 police officers break out in Shishou, China, over the dubious circumstances surrounding the death of a local chef.

events.title: Southern Group of Forces
events.desc: The last Soviet army units in Hungary are withdrawn.

events.title: People's Armed Police
events.desc: The People's Armed Police is de facto founded; It is officially established 10 months later on April 5, 1983

events.title: Nguyễn Cao Kỳ
events.desc: Nguyễn Cao Kỳ becomes Prime Minister of South Vietnam at the head of a military junta; General Nguyễn Văn Thiệu becomes the figurehead chief of state.

events.title: 1945 El Teniente mining accident
events.desc: The Smoke Tragedy left 355 workers dead in the underground copper mine of El Teniente, Chile.

events.title: Herzegovina uprising (1875–1877)
events.desc: The Herzegovinian rebellion against the Ottoman Empire begins.

events.title: Battle of Seven Oaks
events.desc: Battle of Seven Oaks between North West Company and Hudson's Bay Company, near Winnipeg, Manitoba, Canada.

events.title: King's Chapel
events.desc: The Boston King's Chapel adopts James Freeman's revised prayer book, without the Nicene Creed, establishing it as the first Unitarian congregation in the United States.

events.title: 1718 Tongwei–Gansu earthquake
events.desc: At least 73,000 people died in the 1718 Tongwei–Gansu earthquake due to landslides in the Qing dynasty.

births.name: Pier Angeli
births.line: Italian actress, twin sister to Marisa Pavan (died 1971)

deaths.text: Carlos Monsiváis, Mexican writer, journalist and political activist
J. M. Barrie, Scottish novelist and playwright
Benjamin Tasker Sr., American soldier and politician, 10th Colonial Governor of Maryland

observances.text: Christian feast day: Deodatus (or Didier) of Nevers (or of Jointures)
Christian feast day: Gervasius and Protasius (Catholic Church)
